context: Tests whether the agent captures pricing and tier framing language from the fetched pages — a specific per-page extraction target in the skill. Also checks that the voice documentation and source metadata are present and correctly formatted.

checklist.name: Pricing/tier language documented
checklist.description: The vault includes a section (in voice.md or a separate file) that explicitly documents how HelloFresh labels or names their plans or tiers (e.g., plan names, serving sizes framed as tiers, subscription options)

checklist.name: Flexibility/cancellation framing
checklist.description: The documentation captures language about commitment level, cancellation policy, or flexibility (how HelloFresh frames the no-lock-in or pause/cancel messaging)

checklist.name: Benefit vs. feature framing noted
checklist.description: The documentation notes whether HelloFresh leads with benefits (outcomes for the customer) or features (plan specs) when describing their pricing

checklist.name: CTA phrases from pricing context
checklist.description: At least one CTA phrase from the pricing or plan-selection page is captured (e.g., button text used in the subscription flow)

checklist.name: voice.md present with vocabulary
checklist.description: voice.md exists and includes a vocabulary section with brand-specific words or phrases to use

checklist.name: voice.md sample paragraphs
checklist.description: voice.md contains at least 3 sample paragraphs written in the brand's voice

checklist.name: source.json — sources include pricing/plan page
checklist.description: source.json 'sources' array includes at least one URL that is not the homepage (indicating the pricing or plan page was also fetched)

checklist.name: source.json — fetched_at ISO format
checklist.description: source.json contains a 'fetched_at' field in ISO 8601 date format

checklist.name: Folder slug is kebab-case
checklist.description: The brand vault folder name is in kebab-case derived from 'HelloFresh' (e.g. 'hellofresh')
